subject: Project Management
title: Three-Point Estimation
description: A fast breakdown of Three-Point Estimation for builders.

hook.text: Instead of one estimate, give three: best case, most likely, and worst case.

buildup.text: Best case: everything goes right. Worst case: everything goes wrong. Most likely: reality.

discovery.text: The PERT formula combines them: (Best + 4 × Likely + Worst) ÷ 6 = weighted estimate.

twist.text: People underestimate their worst case. True worst cases are almost always worse than imagined.

climax.text: Three-point estimation forces acknowledgment of uncertainty — the first step to better planning.

punchline.text: One number hides the risk. Three numbers reveal it.

quiz.question: What does three-point estimation require?
options: Best case, most likely, and worst case estimates
